phase321a_venkatesan.verdict: Venkatesan cross-check: 0 exact + 0 partial = 0/56 (0%) agreement. 56 disagreements. WEAK convergence — readings diverge significantly.

phase321b_kriger.verdict: Uniqueness: 1631/1670 = 97.7% (Kriger claims 98.3% on unicorn seals). CONSISTENT with Kriger's registration-code hypothesis.

tier1_immediate.name: S.K. Venkatesan
tier1_immediate.work: decipher-ivc: Proto-Dravidian logo-syllabic decipherment using DEDR + M77
tier1_immediate.why: Most directly comparable independent work. Uses same data (M77, DEDR). Head-to-head reading comparison possible.
tier1_immediate.action: Share our reading table for comparison. Propose joint validation.

tier1_immediate.name: Ashish Nair
tier1_immediate.why: His multi-metric framework is the most rigorous structural test. We should run his exact code on our decoded corpus.
tier1_immediate.action: Share our decoded corpus for scorecard testing. Propose collaboration.

tier1_immediate.name: Kevin Shaw
tier1_immediate.work: LISSE framework + Harappan Phonetic Constraint Field (2026)
tier1_immediate.why: Different theoretical stance (non-phonetic). Structural comparison valuable.
tier1_immediate.action: Share preprint v3. Discuss structural vs phonetic approaches.

tier2_validation.work: Semasiographic decipherment: fish=gemstone, taxation/licensing model (Nature HSS Comms 2023, SSRN 2021-2025)
tier2_validation.why: Most rigorous semasiographic researcher. Our fish-sign 0/140 finding confirms her compound-only observation. Published in peer-reviewed venue.
tier2_validation.action: Share fish-sign finding + guild-identity formula results. Discuss phonetic vs semasiographic.

tier2_validation.name: Boris Kriger

tier2_validation.why: His registration-code model partly overlaps with our guild-identity reading. First positional entropy analysis.

tier2_validation.why: Computational tool with n-gram modeling and naive Bayes classifier. Could test our readings.
tier2_validation.action: Propose testing our reading model in their interactive framework.
